project.name: mgc - Microsoft Graph CLI
project.description: A cross-platform, agent-friendly command-line tool for the Microsoft Graph API. Inspired by 'gh' and 'az', designed for both human operators and automated agents.

phases.name: Phase 1: Architecture & Foundation
phases.description: Design the CLI architecture and initialize the Go project with all scaffolding

tasks.title: [ARCH] Design mgc CLI architecture and create ADRs
tasks.body:
## Objective

As the architect, design the overall architecture for the `mgc` (Microsoft Graph CLI) tool and document key architectural decisions.

## Requirements

1. **Update `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`** with:
   - High-level component diagram (ASCII art)
   - Layer descriptions: CLI layer (cobra), client layer (HTTP), auth layer (OAuth2)
   - Data flow: user → command → auth → graph API → output formatter
   - Output format strategy (JSON, table, YAML, TSV)
   - Error handling strategy
   - Configuration file location (`~/.config/mgc/`)
   - Cross-platform considerations (Windows/macOS/Linux)

2. **Create `docs/adr/` directory** with ADR files:
   - `001-use-cobra-for-cli.md` - Why cobra over other CLI frameworks
   - `002-oauth2-device-flow.md` - Auth approach for headless/agent use
   - `003-output-formats.md` - Why support JSON/table/YAML/TSV
   - `004-go-language-choice.md` - Cross-platform single binary

3. **Review existing `mgc/` scaffold** and ensure architecture aligns

## Acceptance Criteria
- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` has all required sections
- At least 3 ADR files created in `docs/adr/`
- All files use clear, concise English
- Architecture supports agent-friendly usage (structured JSON output, no interactive prompts unless `--interactive` flag)

## Context
- The `mgc/` directory has the initial Go scaffold
- See `README.md` for project overview
- Reference: https://www.powershellgallery.com/packages/Microsoft.Graph/2.35.1 for API scope
- Design for parity with popular Graph API operations: users, groups, mail, calendar, files

tasks.title: [DEV] Initialize Go module and cobra CLI framework for mgc
tasks.body:
## Objective

Set up the complete Go project scaffold for the `mgc` CLI tool in the `mgc/` directory.

## Requirements

All code goes in the `mgc/` directory. Initialize and flesh out:

### 1. `mgc/go.mod`
```
module github.com/chenxizhang/testagent/mgc

go 1.22
```
Add dependencies:
- `github.com/spf13/cobra` - CLI framework
- `github.com/spf13/viper` - Configuration management
- `github.com/fatih/color` - Colored output
- `github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter` - Table output
- `gopkg.in/yaml.v3` - YAML output

### 2. `mgc/main.go`
Entry point that calls `cmd.Execute()`.

### 3. `mgc/cmd/root.go`
- Root `mgc` command with persistent flags:
  - `--output` / `-o` : output format (`json`, `table`, `yaml`, `tsv`), default `table`
  - `--query` / `-q` : JMESPath query string to filter output
  - `--tenant` : Azure tenant ID (overrides config)
  - `--debug` : Enable debug logging
- Version command: `mgc version`
- Config command: `mgc config list`, `mgc config set <key> <value>`

### 4. `mgc/cmd/auth/`
- `mgc/cmd/auth/auth.go` - `mgc auth` subcommand group
- `mgc/cmd/auth/login.go` - `mgc auth login [--tenant <id>]` (device flow)
- `mgc/cmd/auth/logout.go` - `mgc auth logout`
- `mgc/cmd/auth/status.go` - `mgc auth status` (show current auth)

### 5. `mgc/internal/output/format.go`
- `Printer` struct with `Print(data interface{}, format string)` method
- Support: json (pretty), table, yaml, tsv
- Auto-detect slice vs single object for table headers

### 6. `mgc/internal/auth/auth.go`
- `AuthManager` struct
- `Login(tenantID string) error` - Initiate device flow, print user code
- `GetToken() (string, error)` - Return cached or refreshed token
- `Logout() error` - Clear cached credentials
- Store tokens in `~/.config/mgc/credentials.json`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cd mgc && go build ./...` succeeds with no errors
- `go test ./...` runs without panics (even with 0 tests)
- `mgc --help` shows proper help text
- `mgc version` shows version string
- Code follows Go standard conventions (`gofmt` formatted)

## Notes
- Use `github.com/spf13/cobra` for all commands
- The tool should be cross-platform: no OS-specific syscalls in cmd layer
- Keep each file under 200 lines

tasks.title: [DEV] Set up GitHub Actions CI pipeline for mgc Go project
tasks.body:
## Objective

The `.github/workflows/build-test.yml` CI workflow already exists. Verify it correctly handles the `mgc/` subdirectory structure and enhance it.

## Requirements

1. **Verify/update `.github/workflows/build-test.yml`** to:
   - Run `go build ./...` in the `mgc/` directory
   - Run `go test ./... -v -race` in `mgc/`
   - Run `go vet ./...` in `mgc/`
   - Run `staticcheck ./...` (if available)
   - Cache Go modules properly
   - Run on `push` to any branch AND `pull_request`
   - Report build status in PR comment

2. **Create `mgc/.golangci.yml`** with linting configuration:
   - Enable: `gofmt`, `govet`, `errcheck`, `staticcheck`, `unused`
   - Disable: `exhaustive`, `wrapcheck`
   - Max issues per linter: 50

3. **Create `mgc/Makefile`** with targets:
   - `make build` → `go build -o bin/mgc ./`
   - `make test` → `go test ./... -race -cover`
   - `make lint` → `golangci-lint run`
   - `make clean` → remove `bin/`
   - `make cross-build` → build for linux/amd64, darwin/amd64, darwin/arm64, windows/amd64

4. **Create `mgc/.gitignore`** excluding `bin/`, `*.exe`, `dist/`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `make build` completes successfully
- `make test` completes without errors
- CI workflow is syntactically valid YAML
- Cross-build targets all complete successfully

## Notes
- Working directory for all go commands is `mgc/`
- The Go version to use: 1.22 or later
- Store binaries in `mgc/bin/` (gitignored)

phases.name: Phase 2: Authentication & Graph Client
phases.description: Implement the authentication layer and base Microsoft Graph API HTTP client

tasks.title: [DEV] Implement Microsoft Graph API HTTP client with retry and error handling
tasks.body:
## Objective

Implement the base Microsoft Graph API HTTP client in `mgc/internal/client/`.

## Requirements

### `mgc/internal/client/graph.go`

```go
type Client struct {
    BaseURL    string // https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0
    HTTPClient *http.Client
    Auth       auth.AuthManager
    Debug      bool
}

func New(authMgr auth.AuthManager) *Client
func (c *Client) Get(path string, params url.Values) (*Response, error)
func (c *Client) Post(path string, body interface{}) (*Response, error)
func (c *Client) Patch(path string, body interface{}) (*Response, error)
func (c *Client) Delete(path string) error
```

### Features:
1. **Automatic token injection**: Calls `auth.GetToken()` and sets `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header
2. **Retry logic**: Exponential backoff on 429 (rate limit) and 503 errors, max 3 retries
3. **Error handling**: Parse Microsoft Graph error responses (`error.code`, `error.message`) into typed errors
4. **OData pagination**: Helper method `GetAll(path, params)` that follows `@odata.nextLink`
5. **Debug logging**: When `Debug=true`, log request URL and response status to stderr
6. **Timeout**: 30 second timeout on all requests

### `mgc/internal/client/response.go`
```go
type Response struct {
    StatusCode int
    Body       []byte
    Value      []json.RawMessage // for OData collections
    NextLink   string
}

func (r *Response) Unmarshal(v interface{}) error
func (r *Response) UnmarshalValue(v interface{}) error // unmarshals .value array
```

### `mgc/internal/client/errors.go`
```go
type GraphError struct {
    Code    string
    Message string
    Status  int
}
func (e *GraphError) Error() string
func IsNotFound(err error) bool
func IsUnauthorized(err error) bool
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All types compile without errors
- Unit tests in `mgc/internal/client/graph_test.go` with mock HTTP server
- Tests cover: successful GET, error response, pagination, retry on 429
- `go test ./internal/client/...` passes

## Notes
- Use only Go standard library for HTTP (no external HTTP clients)
- Token refresh should be transparent to callers
- The client should work with both delegated (user) and app-only authentication

tasks.title: [DEV] Implement device flow OAuth2 authentication with token caching
tasks.body:
## Objective

Implement the full OAuth2 device flow authentication in `mgc/internal/auth/auth.go`.

## Requirements

### Device Flow (for `mgc auth login`)
1. Call `https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/devicecode`
2. Display user code and verification URL to user
3. Poll `https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/token` until approved or timeout
4. Save access_token + refresh_token + expiry to `~/.config/mgc/credentials.json`

### Token Management
1. `GetToken() (string, error)` - Return cached token if not expired, else refresh using refresh_token
2. Store credentials encrypted using XOR with machine-specific key (hostname+username), base64 encoded
3. Support multiple tenant profiles in `credentials.json`

### Config File
`~/.config/mgc/config.json`:
```json
{
  "default_tenant": "...",
  "client_id": "14d82eec-204b-4c2f-b7e8-296a70dab67e",
  "default_output": "table"
}
```

Use the public Microsoft Graph Explorer client_id: `14d82eec-204b-4c2f-b7e8-296a70dab67e`

### Scopes to request:
`https://graph.microsoft.com/.default offline_access`

### Files to create:
- `mgc/internal/auth/auth.go` - Main AuthManager implementation
- `mgc/internal/auth/token.go` - Token storage and retrieval
- `mgc/internal/auth/device_flow.go` - Device flow implementation
- `mgc/internal/auth/auth_test.go` - Unit tests with mocked HTTP

## Acceptance Criteria
- `mgc auth login` initiates device flow and prints code to user
- `mgc auth status` shows current authenticated user (calls `/me`)
- `mgc auth logout` removes cached credentials
- Token refresh works transparently
- Unit tests pass: `go test ./internal/auth/...`

## Notes
- NEVER store tokens in plain text in the codebase
- Cross-platform: use `~/.config/mgc/` on Linux/Mac, `%APPDATA%\mgc\` on Windows
- No external auth libraries - implement device flow manually with net/http

tasks.title: [TEST] Write unit tests for authentication and Graph client modules
tasks.body:
## Objective

Write comprehensive unit tests for the authentication and Graph API client modules.

## Requirements

### 1. `mgc/internal/auth/auth_test.go`
Test cases:
- `TestDeviceFlowLogin` - Mock MSAL endpoint, verify device code display
- `TestTokenRefresh` - Create expired token, mock refresh endpoint, verify new token
- `TestTokenCache` - Write token to disk, read back, verify content
- `TestLogout` - Verify credentials file is deleted
- `TestGetTokenExpired` - Simulate expired token, verify refresh is called

### 2. `mgc/internal/client/graph_test.go`
Test cases:
- `TestGetSuccess` - Mock Graph API returning 200 with JSON body
- `TestGetUnauthorized` - Mock 401, verify GraphError type
- `TestGetRateLimit` - Mock 429 with Retry-After header, verify retry
- `TestGetPagination` - Mock two pages with @odata.nextLink, verify GetAll returns all items
- `TestGetNotFound` - Mock 404, verify IsNotFound returns true
- `TestPostSuccess` - Mock 201 Created response

### 3. Create `mgc/internal/testutil/testutil.go`
Shared test utilities:
```go
func NewMockGraphServer(responses []MockResponse) *httptest.Server
func NewMockAuthServer() *httptest.Server
func NewTestClient(serverURL string) *client.Client
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- `go test ./... -race` passes with 0 failures
- Test coverage ≥ 70% for `internal/auth` and `internal/client` packages
- Tests use `httptest.NewServer` for mocking, no external calls
- Each test function has a clear docstring comment

## Notes
- Use Go's standard `testing` package, no external test frameworks
- Use table-driven tests where appropriate
- Run with `go test ./... -v -coverprofile=coverage.out` and check coverage

phases.name: Phase 3: Users & Groups Commands
phases.description: Implement the core users and groups management commands

tasks.title: [DEV] Implement mgc users commands (list, get, create, update, delete)
tasks.body:
## Objective

Implement all `mgc users` subcommands in `mgc/cmd/users/`.

## Commands to implement

### `mgc users list`
- Flags: `--filter <odata-filter>`, `--select <fields>`, `--top <int>`, `--all` (follow pagination)
- Calls `GET /users?$filter=...&$select=...&$top=...`
- Default columns for table output: `id`, `displayName`, `userPrincipalName`, `mail`, `jobTitle`
- Example: `mgc users list --filter "startsWith(displayName,'A')" -o json`

### `mgc users get <id-or-upn>`
- Calls `GET /users/{id}`
- Shows all user properties in table
- Example: `mgc users get user@contoso.com`

### `mgc users create`
- Flags: `--display-name`, `--upn`, `--password`, `--force-change-password`
- Calls `POST /users`
- Example: `mgc users create --display-name "John Doe" --upn john@contoso.com --password P@ssw0rd`

### `mgc users update <id-or-upn>`
- Flags: `--display-name`, `--job-title`, `--department`, `--mobile-phone`
- Calls `PATCH /users/{id}`

### `mgc users delete <id-or-upn>`
- Calls `DELETE /users/{id}`
- Requires `--yes` flag to confirm, or prompts if terminal is interactive

## File structure:
- `mgc/cmd/users/users.go` - Parent command, registers subcommands
- `mgc/cmd/users/list.go`
- `mgc/cmd/users/get.go`
- `mgc/cmd/users/create.go`
- `mgc/cmd/users/update.go`
- `mgc/cmd/users/delete.go`

## Acceptance Criteria
- All commands compile and have proper `--help` text
- Commands registered in `cmd/root.go`
- `go build ./...` passes
- Output respects `--output` flag (json, table, yaml, tsv)

## Notes
- Use `mgc/internal/client` for all API calls
- Use `mgc/internal/output` for all output formatting
- Error messages should include the Graph error code when available

tasks.title: [DEV] Implement mgc groups commands (list, get, create, members)
tasks.body:
## Objective

Implement all `mgc groups` subcommands in `mgc/cmd/groups/`.

## Commands to implement

### `mgc groups list`
- Flags: `--filter <odata-filter>`, `--select <fields>`, `--top <int>`, `--all`
- Calls `GET /groups?$filter=...`
- Default columns: `id`, `displayName`, `mail`, `groupTypes`
- Example: `mgc groups list --filter "startsWith(displayName,'Dev')" -o table`

### `mgc groups get <id>`
- Calls `GET /groups/{id}`

### `mgc groups create`
- Flags: `--display-name`, `--mail-nickname`, `--description`, `--mail-enabled`, `--security-enabled`
- Calls `POST /groups`
- Example: `mgc groups create --display-name "DevTeam" --mail-nickname devteam --security-enabled`

### `mgc groups members`
- `mgc groups members list <group-id>` - `GET /groups/{id}/members`
- `mgc groups members add <group-id> --member <user-id>` - `POST /groups/{id}/members/$ref`
- `mgc groups members remove <group-id> --member <user-id>` - `DELETE /groups/{id}/members/{member-id}/$ref`

### `mgc groups owners`
- `mgc groups owners list <group-id>` - `GET /groups/{id}/owners`

## File structure:
- `mgc/cmd/groups/groups.go`
- `mgc/cmd/groups/list.go`
- `mgc/cmd/groups/get.go`
- `mgc/cmd/groups/create.go`
- `mgc/cmd/groups/members.go`
- `mgc/cmd/groups/owners.go`

## Acceptance Criteria
- All commands compile with `go build ./...`
- `mgc groups --help` shows all subcommands
- Commands respect `--output` and `--query` global flags
- Registered in root command

## Notes
- Follow the same pattern as `cmd/users/`
- Use `internal/client` and `internal/output` packages
- `--all` flag should auto-paginate using `GetAll()`

tasks.title: [TEST] Write integration tests for users and groups commands
tasks.body:
## Objective

Write integration tests for `mgc users` and `mgc groups` commands using a mock Graph API server.

## Requirements

### 1. `mgc/cmd/users/users_test.go`
Test cases (all using mock HTTP server):
- `TestUsersList` - Verify correct API call, correct output format
- `TestUsersListWithFilter` - Verify filter is passed in query string
- `TestUsersGet` - Verify single user output
- `TestUsersCreate` - Verify POST body is correct
- `TestUsersDelete_WithConfirm` - Verify `--yes` flag skips prompt
- `TestUsersListPagination` - Verify `--all` follows nextLink

### 2. `mgc/cmd/groups/groups_test.go`
Test cases:
- `TestGroupsList` - Verify correct output
- `TestGroupsCreate` - Verify POST body
- `TestGroupsMembersList` - Verify members endpoint called
- `TestGroupsMembersAdd` - Verify $ref POST

### 3. `mgc/cmd/cmd_test.go`
- `TestRootHelp` - Verify `mgc --help` output contains expected commands
- `TestVersionCmd` - Verify version string format
- `TestOutputFormats` - Verify json/table/yaml output for same data

## Test Pattern
Use a helper that replaces the client base URL with mock server URL:
```go
func withMockServer(responses []testutil.MockResponse, fn func(cmd *cobra.Command)) {
    server := testutil.NewMockGraphServer(responses)
    defer server.Close()
    // Set client base URL to server.URL
    fn(rootCmd)
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- `go test ./cmd/...` passes
- Coverage ≥ 60% for cmd packages
- All tests are independent (no shared state between tests)

## Notes
- Capture stdout using `bytes.Buffer` and `cmd.SetOut()`
- Use `cobra.Command.Execute()` to run commands in tests
- Mock responses should be realistic Graph API JSON

phases.name: Phase 4: Mail & Calendar + Release v0.1.0
phases.description: Implement mail and calendar commands, then release v0.1.0

tasks.title: [DEV] Implement mgc mail commands (list, read, send)
tasks.body:
## Objective

Implement `mgc mail` commands for reading and sending emails via Microsoft Graph.

## Commands to implement

### `mgc mail list`
- Flags: `--folder <name>` (inbox, sentitems, drafts), `--top <int>`, `--all`, `--unread`
- Calls `GET /me/mailFolders/{folder}/messages`
- Default columns: `id`, `subject`, `from.emailAddress.name`, `receivedDateTime`, `isRead`
- Example: `mgc mail list --folder inbox --top 10 --unread`

### `mgc mail read <message-id>`
- Calls `GET /me/messages/{id}`
- Shows full message body (text or HTML rendered as plain text)

### `mgc mail send`
- Flags: `--to <email>`, `--subject <text>`, `--body <text>`, `--body-file <path>`, `--cc <email>`
- Calls `POST /me/sendMail`
- Example: `mgc mail send --to user@contoso.com --subject "Hello" --body "World"`

### `mgc mail folders list`
- Calls `GET /me/mailFolders`

## File structure:
- `mgc/cmd/mail/mail.go`
- `mgc/cmd/mail/list.go`
- `mgc/cmd/mail/read.go`
- `mgc/cmd/mail/send.go`
- `mgc/cmd/mail/folders.go`

## Acceptance Criteria
- All commands compile
- `mgc mail --help` shows all subcommands
- `go build ./...` passes
- Output respects global `--output` flag

## Notes
- HTML body should be stripped to plain text for table/yaml output
- Use `golang.org/x/net/html` for HTML stripping if needed
- Add to root command

tasks.title: [DEV] Implement mgc calendar commands (events list, create, delete)
tasks.body:
## Objective

Implement `mgc calendar` commands for calendar management via Microsoft Graph.

## Commands to implement

### `mgc calendar events list`
- Flags: `--start <datetime>`, `--end <datetime>`, `--top <int>`, `--all`
- Calls `GET /me/events?$orderby=start/dateTime&startDateTime=...&endDateTime=...`
- Default columns: `id`, `subject`, `start.dateTime`, `end.dateTime`, `organizer.emailAddress.name`
- Example: `mgc calendar events list --start 2026-03-10T00:00:00Z --end 2026-03-17T00:00:00Z`

### `mgc calendar events get <event-id>`
- Calls `GET /me/events/{id}`
- Shows all event details

### `mgc calendar events create`
- Flags: `--subject`, `--start`, `--end`, `--timezone`, `--attendees <emails>`, `--location`, `--body`
- Calls `POST /me/events`
- Example: `mgc calendar events create --subject "Team Meeting" --start 2026-03-15T10:00:00 --end 2026-03-15T11:00:00 --timezone "UTC"`

### `mgc calendar events delete <event-id>`
- Calls `DELETE /me/events/{id}`
- Requires `--yes` flag

### `mgc calendar calendars list`
- Calls `GET /me/calendars`

## File structure:
- `mgc/cmd/calendar/calendar.go`
- `mgc/cmd/calendar/events.go`
- `mgc/cmd/calendar/list.go`
- `mgc/cmd/calendar/create.go`
- `mgc/cmd/calendar/delete.go`
- `mgc/cmd/calendar/calendars.go`

## Acceptance Criteria
- All commands compile
- `mgc calendar --help` shows all subcommands
- Datetime flags accept ISO 8601 format
- `go build ./...` passes

## Notes
- Add to root command
- DateTime parsing should be flexible (accept both RFC3339 and `2006-01-02T15:04:05` formats)
- Use `time.Parse` with multiple formats

tasks.title: [TEST] Write tests for mail and calendar commands + E2E test suite
tasks.body:
## Objective

Write tests for mail and calendar commands, and create an end-to-end test suite document.

## Requirements

### 1. `mgc/cmd/mail/mail_test.go`
- `TestMailListInbox` - Verify inbox list call
- `TestMailSend` - Verify send POST body format
- `TestMailRead` - Verify single message retrieval

### 2. `mgc/cmd/calendar/calendar_test.go`
- `TestCalendarEventsList` - Verify events list with date filters
- `TestCalendarEventCreate` - Verify POST body for event creation
- `TestCalendarEventDelete` - Verify DELETE request

### 3. `mgc/docs/testing-guide.md`
Create a testing guide:
- How to run unit tests: `cd mgc && go test ./... -race`
- How to run with coverage: `go test ./... -coverprofile=coverage.out && go tool cover -html=coverage.out`
- How to do manual testing with a real tenant (steps for device flow)
- E2E test checklist for manual validation before release

### 4. Update `mgc/Makefile`
Add: `make test-coverage` target that generates HTML coverage report

## Acceptance Criteria
- `go test ./...` passes with 0 failures
- Coverage report can be generated
- Testing guide is clear and actionable

## Notes
- Follow same test patterns established in Phase 2 and 3 tests
- Mock all HTTP calls - no real network calls in tests

tasks.title: [RELEASE] Prepare and tag v0.1.0 release of mgc CLI
tasks.body:
## Objective

Prepare the v0.1.0 release of the `mgc` CLI tool.

## Requirements

### 1. Update `mgc/main.go` version constant
```go
const Version = "0.1.0"
```

### 2. Create `CHANGELOG.md` in root
```markdown
# Changelog

## [0.1.0] - 2026-03-XX

### Added
- `mgc auth login` - OAuth2 device flow authentication
- `mgc auth status` - Show current authentication
- `mgc auth logout` - Clear credentials
- `mgc users` - User management commands (list, get, create, update, delete)
- `mgc groups` - Group management commands (list, get, create, members, owners)
- `mgc mail` - Email commands (list, read, send)
- `mgc calendar` - Calendar commands (events list, get, create, delete)
- Multiple output formats: `--output json|table|yaml|tsv`
- Cross-platform support: Linux, macOS, Windows
```

### 3. Ensure `mgc/Makefile` `cross-build` target works:
```makefile
cross-build:
	GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build -ldflags="-X main.Version=$(VERSION)" -o bin/mgc-linux-amd64 .
	GOOS=darwin GOARCH=amd64 go build -ldflags="-X main.Version=$(VERSION)" -o bin/mgc-darwin-amd64 .
	GOOS=darwin GOARCH=arm64 go build -ldflags="-X main.Version=$(VERSION)" -o bin/mgc-darwin-arm64 .
	GOOS=windows GOARCH=amd64 go build -ldflags="-X main.Version=$(VERSION)" -o bin/mgc-windows-amd64.exe .
```

### 4. Verify all go tests pass:
`cd mgc && go test ./... -race`

### 5. Update `README.md` Installation section with binary download instructions

## Acceptance Criteria
- `CHANGELOG.md` exists with v0.1.0 entry
- Version constant is set to `0.1.0`
- `cross-build` target produces 4 binaries
- All tests pass
- README has installation instructions

## Trigger release
After this PR is merged, the auto-release workflow will create the GitHub release automatically.

## Notes
- Do NOT manually create the git tag - the `auto-release.yml` workflow does this
- Just update the version constant and changelog, then commit

phases.name: Phase 5: Advanced Features & v0.2.0
phases.description: Add advanced features: JMESPath filtering, OneDrive files, Teams, batch operations

tasks.title: [DEV] Implement JMESPath query filtering for all commands
tasks.body:
## Objective

Implement `--query` flag support using JMESPath to filter and transform output from any command.

## Requirements

### Add dependency
`go get github.com/jmespath/go-jmespath`

### Update `mgc/internal/output/format.go`
- After getting JSON data, apply JMESPath query if `--query` flag is set
- Then format the result according to `--output` flag

### Examples
```bash
# Get just display names
mgc users list --query '[].displayName' -o json

# Filter users in Engineering
mgc users list --query "[?department=='Engineering'].{name: displayName, email: mail}"

# Get first calendar event subject
mgc calendar events list --query '[0].subject'
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- `--query` flag works on all commands that return data
- Works with all output formats
- Invalid JMESPath expression shows helpful error message
- Tests added for query filtering in `output/format_test.go`

tasks.title: [DEV] Implement mgc files commands (OneDrive)
tasks.body:
## Objective

Implement `mgc files` commands for OneDrive file management.

## Commands to implement

### `mgc files list [path]`
- Lists files in OneDrive root or specified path
- Calls `GET /me/drive/root/children` or `GET /me/drive/root:/{path}:/children`
- Default columns: `id`, `name`, `size`, `lastModifiedDateTime`, `folder`

### `mgc files get <path>`
- Gets file metadata: `GET /me/drive/root:/{path}`

### `mgc files download <path> [--output-file <local-path>]`
- Downloads file content: `GET /me/drive/root:/{path}:/content`
- Saves to local file or stdout

### `mgc files upload <local-path> [--dest <remote-path>]`
- Uploads file: `PUT /me/drive/root:/{remotePath}:/content`
- For files < 4MB

### `mgc files delete <path>`
- Deletes file: `DELETE /me/drive/root:/{path}`

## File structure:
- `mgc/cmd/files/files.go`
- `mgc/cmd/files/list.go`
- `mgc/cmd/files/download.go`
- `mgc/cmd/files/upload.go`
- `mgc/cmd/files/delete.go`

## Acceptance Criteria
- All commands compile
- Upload/download tested with mock server
- `go build ./...` passes

tasks.title: [ARCH] Code review: Review Phase 3-5 implementation quality
tasks.body:
## Objective

Conduct an architectural code review of all code implemented in Phases 3-5.

## Review Checklist

### Code Quality
- [ ] All public functions have godoc comments
- [ ] Error handling is consistent (wrap with context)
- [ ] No `panic()` in non-test code
- [ ] Context is passed through for cancellation support
- [ ] No hardcoded credentials or tenant IDs

### API Design
- [ ] Command flags are consistent across all commands
- [ ] Output format is consistent
- [ ] Error messages are helpful and actionable
- [ ] `--help` text is accurate

### Testing
- [ ] Critical paths have test coverage
- [ ] Tests are independent
- [ ] No real network calls in tests

## Deliverables

1. **Create `docs/code-review-phase-3-5.md`** with findings and recommendations
2. **Fix any critical issues** found during review (update code directly)
3. **Update `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`** if actual implementation differs from design

## Acceptance Criteria
- Review document created with specific findings per package
- All critical issues (security, crash-risk) fixed in same PR
- Architecture doc reflects actual implementation

phases.name: Phase 6: Polish, Documentation & v1.0.0 Release
phases.description: Final polish, complete documentation, and release v1.0.0

tasks.title: [DEV] Add shell completion and interactive prompts
tasks.body:
## Objective

Add shell completion support and improve interactive UX.

## Requirements

### 1. Shell completion
Cobra provides this natively:
```go
rootCmd.AddCommand(completionCmd)
```
Add `mgc completion bash|zsh|fish|powershell` command.

### 2. Install script
Create `scripts/install.sh` that:
- Detects OS and architecture
- Downloads appropriate binary from GitHub Releases
- Installs to `/usr/local/bin/mgc` (Linux/Mac) or `C:\Program Files\mgc\` (Windows)
- Sets up shell completion

### 3. `mgc init` command
Interactive setup wizard:
- Prompts for tenant ID (or discovers from environment)
- Runs `auth login`
- Tests connection with `mgc users get me`
- Shows success message

## Acceptance Criteria
- `mgc completion bash` outputs valid bash completion script
- Install script works on Ubuntu
- `mgc init` runs without errors

tasks.title: [DEV] Complete README with full usage examples and badges
tasks.body:
## Objective

Update `README.md` with comprehensive documentation.

## Requirements

### Sections to add/update:
1. **Badges**: build status, version, license, Go version
2. **Quick Start**: 3-step getting started (install, login, first command)
3. **Installation**: All platforms + install script
4. **Authentication**: Explain device flow, tenant configuration
5. **Commands Reference**: Every command with examples
   - `auth`, `users`, `groups`, `mail`, `calendar`, `files`
6. **Output Formats**: Examples of json/table/yaml/tsv for same command
7. **Agent-Friendly Usage**: How to use mgc in scripts and agents
   - `eval $(mgc auth login --format env-vars)`
   - `mgc users list -o json | jq '.[] | .userPrincipalName'`
   - `mgc users list --query '[].userPrincipalName' -o tsv`
8. **Configuration**: `~/.config/mgc/config.json` reference
9. **Contributing**: How to contribute (for agents and humans)
10. **License**: MIT

## Acceptance Criteria
- README has all 10 sections
- Every command has at least one example
- Agent-friendly section demonstrates JMESPath querying
- README renders correctly as Markdown

tasks.title: [RELEASE] Prepare v1.0.0 release - final testing and changelog
tasks.body:
## Objective

Prepare the v1.0.0 release of the `mgc` CLI.

## Requirements

### 1. Final test pass
Run and verify:
```bash
cd mgc
go test ./... -race -count=1
go vet ./...
go build ./...
```
Fix any remaining test failures or build errors.

### 2. Update `mgc/main.go`
```go
const Version = "1.0.0"
```

### 3. Update `CHANGELOG.md`
Add v1.0.0 section with all features added since v0.1.0:
- JMESPath query filtering
- OneDrive files commands
- Shell completion
- `mgc init` wizard
- Code review improvements from Phase 5

### 4. Create `SECURITY.md`
- Responsible disclosure policy
- Note that credentials are stored encrypted
- Supported versions

### 5. Create `.github/CODEOWNERS`
```
* @chenxizhang
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All tests pass
- Version is `1.0.0`
- CHANGELOG is complete
- `go build ./...` produces working binary

## Notes
- After this PR merges to main, the auto-release workflow tags v1.0.0 automatically
- The workflow builds cross-platform binaries and attaches to the release
